name: Set-TraceSource

options.name: -Debugger
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet sends the trace output to the debugger. You can view the output in any user-mode or kernel mode debugger or in Microsoft Visual Studio. This parameter also selects the default trace listener.

name: -FilePath
-PSPath
-Path
options.description: Specifies a file that this cmdlet sends the trace output to. This parameter also selects the file trace listener. If you use this parameter to start the trace, use the RemoveFileListener parameter to stop the trace.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet overwrites a read-only file. Use with the FilePath parameter.

options.name: -ListenerOption
options.description: Specifies optional data to the prefix of each trace message in the output. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies which components are traced. Enter the name of the trace source of each component. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -Option
options.description: Specifies the type of events that are traced. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Returns an object representing the item with which you are working. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

options.name: -PSHost
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet sends the trace output to the PowerShell host. This parameter also selects the PSHost trace listener.

options.name: -RemoveFileListener
options.description: Stops the trace by removing the file trace listener associated with the specified file. Enter the path and file name of the trace output file.

options.name: -RemoveListener
options.description: Stops the trace by removing the trace listener.

args.name: -Name
args.description: Specifies which components are traced. Enter the name of the trace source of each component. Wildcards are permitted.

args.name: -Option
args.description: Specifies the type of events that are traced. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

description: Configures, starts, and stops a trace of PowerShell components.
